title: Review of Tenku no Girlfriend 1st
wikitext:
[]: Review of Tenku no Girlfriend 1st             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Mon Aug 22, 2005 8:37 pm Misato walks out of boy's bathroom followed by Kaji in a towel. o.0 What a horrifying bad game! And that comes from an Eva freak. Ask anyone, they'll tell you. The game is pointless and feels like a filler episode where they got bored so made a horriblly bad quality game from it. I finished every one of the five endings(including one where you see a porno magazine in Misato's room) in less then a day. Tenku no Girlfriend has no relation at all to the sequel by a similar name. In this game, a new girl named Mana Kirshima comes to Shinji's school. Mana is a pilot to an alternative to Evangelion. Similar to Jet Alone. Mana and Shinji become attracted and they head to Misato's house. Afterwords, Shinji stays up all night thinking. In the morning, Shinji calls Mana. Mana asks him on a date and you can decide wether you want to date Rei, Misato, Asuka, Mana or Kaji... What is with Gainax and wierd Eva dating sims. No matter who you chose, Asuka gets really jealous(Well, except her of course) and starts giving you the cold shoulder all day. There are only two intresting dates, Hence I shall only bother describing those two. If you date Misato, she makes Shinji drink beer and in the night he is drunk. He is very funny when he is drunk. Misato yells at him for not finishing more than one can ;) Then if you date Asuka, you go to the lake but then a storm hits and you are trapped at a bus stop. Asuka and Shinji snuggle up in a cute scene. And Shinji promises that he will never leave her. Asuka hears her mother tell her that she is no longer alone. That she will be with Shinji forever. Alright, then the next day you wake up and Gendo creates a fake angel attack. If you dated Mana, she will follow you into Nerv. If you choose to fight with Rei, then the story will go on with Mana getting taken away by men in black sutis. But if you choose to fight with Asuka. Gendo tells Shinji that an army of angels is coming tp destroy the Earth. Then the game ends. What? What type of horrible ending is this? Well it's the ending. Gainax learned a lot when they released the game I described as near perfect entitled, "Tenku no Girlfriend 2nd"(read the review of that ^^ ) So now for my Review of Tenku no Girlfriend the original. Sound: 6/10 Sound is choppy but it is the original cast. Graphics: 4/10: It really is just a bunch of pictures and scenes taken from the show and repasted together. Plot: 5/10 It was a good idea but it was excuted horribly Gameplay: 5/10 Horrible, it's basically just "click, click, click, click, click." Replayabiltiy: 2/10 I had no fun at all replaying it to get the alternate endings. Overall: Neon Genisis Evangelion: Tenku no Girlfriend gets a rather pathetic 8/18 angels. On the brightside the game was released in the U.S.(Not officially but you can find fan translations here and there) I can post it if requested. Unless you want to learn about Mana Kirshima and see some Shinji/Asuka scenes otherwise avoid like the plague. Note: I recently discovered the English version is just a demo, not the full but I am too lazy to import it.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Mon Aug 22, 2005 8:45 pm Wasn't it Koutetsu (鋼鉄) no Girlfriend?

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Mon Aug 22, 2005 8:48 pm Yes and no. The word written on the title of the first one is Tenku, second is Koutetsu but I like the word Tenku better.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Aug 22, 2005 9:36 pm Waaaaaaaaaaaaaitaminit-- how the hell did you get two other endings? In the morning, Shinji calls Mana. Mana asks him on a date and you can decide wether you want to date Rei, Misato, Asuka, Mana or Kaji... What is with Gainax and wierd Eva dating sims.

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Mon Aug 22, 2005 9:52 pm Er... I took it... http://www.juzzam.com/IronMaiden.ZIP It seems to be a horrible english version... Regardless. I can't import. Now...

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Aug 22, 2005 11:16 pm I didn't say you doctored it, but that portion of the game is illegit--even those two character cells have been recycled from the anime. I'm not sure where I downloaded it from. It was nearly a year ago. That version has obviously been hacked.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Aug 23, 2005 1:39 am Darkbyte wrote: Yes and no. The word written on the title of the first one is Tenku, second is Koutetsu but I like the word Tenku better.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 23, 2005 1:49 am Wow... someone hacked the english translation? HILARIOUS. I can't wait to see this for myself.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Tue Aug 23, 2005 10:19 pm Ohgod, it's so bad. XD I hate Mana. Shinji needs to be with Asuka. And...so very edited. It's not supposed to be the real Tenku no Girlfriend, just a ripoff of it, if you read the FAQ. They tricked me, too. They said you could look at a hentai magazine by clicking on the drawer near the TV and typing 'rouji' for a password (it's RYOJI. Ryoji! Not Rouji.) But it was yuri. ;___; Ahhhhh, my eyes. They also spelled Touji's name 'Todji'. >_>

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Wed Aug 24, 2005 9:31 am Anything that includes Mana can't be any good. This includes fanfics, fanart, doujins, hentai and possibly live-action movies.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Wed Aug 24, 2005 9:40 am Ryo wrote: Anything that includes Mana can't be any good. This includes fanfics, fanart, doujins, hentai and possibly live-action movies.

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Wed Aug 24, 2005 9:54 am Shinji and Asuka! =p

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Wed Aug 24, 2005 10:57 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Ryo wrote: Anything that includes Mana can't be any good. This includes fanfics, fanart, doujins, hentai and possibly live-action movies.

[]:             Quote Post by akda1ndaonly1 » Wed Aug 24, 2005 3:57 pm Wow.. I just tried it and it really, really sucked... I think the only part I liked was that you could listen to all the Eva music.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Aug 24, 2005 4:14 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: But what about RPG's?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Aug 24, 2005 6:35 pm Better explain myself. Mana. As in MP, mana points. RPG's. It's a joke, da ne?
